paper_assessments.rationale: Studies ZBTB16/PLZF, a different family member, and lineage specification, not ZBTB32 or TCR inhibition.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: CD4 TCR-engineered T cells ... significantly inhibited nasopharyngeal cancer growth in mouse models

paper_assessments.rationale: Review of ZBTB family in T cell development/differentiation; no primary ZBTB32 TCR-inhibition evidence.

paper_assessments.rationale: Computational TCR generation/binding prediction; unrelated to ZBTB32 or TCR inhibition.

paper_assessments.phenotype: exhaustion explicitly linked to impaired TCR function

paper_assessments.rationale: Demonstrates impaired TCR signalling in exhaustion but does not involve ZBTB32.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Inhibiting PCSK9 enhances the effectiveness of TCR-T cells and anti-PD-1 immunotherapy

paper_assessments.rationale: Studies Lck/Fyn/ZAP70 in TCR downregulation; no ZBTB32 and not an inhibitory phenotype.

parsed.tier_rationale: The single ZBTB32-specific primary study (PMC5578684) is a mouse loss-of-function experiment affecting CD8+ effector-versus-memory differentiation and effector magnitude, i.e. general activation/differentiation, not proximal TCR signalling inhibition, checkpoint response, anergy or TCR-linked hyporesponsiveness with a mechanistic TCR-inhibition readout. The two ZBTB reviews cannot support higher tiers. All other retrieved primary papers concern unrelated regulators (PTPN22, PCSK9, Lck/Fyn/ZAP70) or generic TCR/exhaustion biology without ZBTB32. No primary study directly links ZBTB32 to an inhibitory TCR phenotype, so evidence remains weak.
